children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which the Assistant Secretary begins awarding grants under , and not before that date, the Assistant Secretary shall establish in the Department of Commerce the Digital Equity Competitive Grant Program (referred to in this section as the “Program”), the purpose of which is to award grants to support efforts to achieve digital equity, promote digital inclusion activities, and spur greater adoption of broadband among covered populations.section 1723(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the identification of groups described in subparagraphs (A) through (H) of located in that State; andsection 1721(8) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the allocation of grant funds within that State for projects in or affecting the State; and

children.label: A
children.text: provides a detailed explanation of how the entity will use any grant amounts awarded under the Program to carry out the purposes of the Program in an efficient and expeditious manner;

children.label: B
children.text: identifies the period in which the applicant will expend the grant funds awarded under the Program;

children.label: i
children.text: a justification for the amount of the grant that the applicant is requesting; and

children.label: ii

children.label: aa

children.label: E
children.text: discloses to the Assistant Secretary the source and amount of other Federal, State, or outside funding sources from which the entity receives, or has applied for, funding for activities or projects to which the application relates; and

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: an assurance that the entity shall follow such additional procedures as the Assistant Secretary may require to ensure that grant funds are used and accounted for in an appropriate manner.

children.heading: Factors considered in award of grants
children.label: 1 Factors considered in award of grants
i
children.text: increase internet access and the adoption of broadband among covered populations to be served by the applicant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not result in unjust enrichment;

children.label: B
children.text: the comparative geographic diversity of the application in relation to other eligible applications; and

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which an application may duplicate or conflict with another program.

children.label: i
children.text: To develop and implement digital inclusion activities that benefit covered populations.

children.label: ii
children.text: To facilitate the adoption of broadband by covered populations in order to provide educational and employment opportunities to those populations.

children.label: I
children.text: training programs for covered populations that cover basic, advanced, and applied skills; or

children.label: II
children.text: other workforce development programs.

children.label: iv
children.text: To make available equipment, instrumentation, networking capability, hardware and software, or digital network technology for broadband services to covered populations at low or no cost.

children.label: v
children.text: To construct, upgrade, expend, or operate new or existing public access computing centers for covered populations through community anchor institutions.

children.label: vi
children.text: To undertake any other project and activity that the Assistant Secretary finds to be consistent with the purposes for which the Program is established.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An entity to which the Assistant Secretary awards a grant under the Program shall use not more than 10 percent of the grant amounts to measure and evaluate the activities supported with the grant amounts.

children.label: I
children.text: in a manner specified by the Assistant Secretary;

children.label: II
children.text: not later than 15 months after the date on which the entity is awarded the grant amounts; and

children.label: III
children.text: annually after the submission described in subclause (II) for any year in which the entity expends grant amounts.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: C Administrative costs
children.text: An entity to which the Assistant Secretary awards a grant under the Program may use not more than 10 percent of the amount of the grant for administrative costs in carrying out any of the activities described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in clause (ii), shall expend the grant amounts during the 4-year period beginning on the date on which the entity is awarded the grant amounts; and

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 1-year period beginning on the date that is 4 years after the date on which the entity is awarded the grant amounts, may continue to measure and evaluate the activities supported with the grant amounts, as required under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Federal share of any project for which the Assistant Secretary awards a grant under the Program may not exceed 90 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: the applicant with respect to the project petitions the Assistant Secretary for the waiver; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Assistant Secretary determines that the petition described in subparagraph (A) demonstrates financial need.

children.label: A
children.text: in accordance with any applicable statute, regulation, and application procedure; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent required under applicable law;

children.label: A
children.text: enforcing any obligation imposed under law on any agency, institution, organization, or other entity that is responsible for carrying out a program to which the grant relates;

children.label: B
children.text: correcting any deficiency in the operation of a program to which the grant relates, as identified through an audit or another monitoring or evaluation procedure; and

children.label: C
children.text: adopting written procedures for the receipt and resolution of complaints alleging a violation of law with respect to a program to which the grant relates;

children.label: A
children.text: of any program that relates to a grant awarded to the entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is carried out by or for the Assistant Secretary or another Federal official;
use fiscal control and fund accounting procedures that ensure the proper disbursement of, and accounting for, any Federal funds that the entity is awarded under the Program;
submit to the Assistant Secretary any reports that may be necessary to enable the Assistant Secretary to perform the duties of the Assistant Secretary under the Program; and

maintain any records and provide any information to the Assistant Secretary, including those records, that the Assistant Secretary determines is necessary to enable the Assistant Secretary to perform the duties of the Assistant Secretary under the Program.

children.heading: Deobligation or termination of grant
children.label: g Deobligation or termination of grant
i
children.text: the grant funds are not being used in a manner that is consistent with the application with respect to the grant submitted by the entity under subsection (c); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the entity is not upholding assurances made by the entity to the Assistant Secretary under subsection (f); and

children.label: B
children.text: determines that the grant is no longer necessary to achieve the original purpose for which Assistant Secretary awarded the grant; and1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.1

with respect to any grant funds that the Assistant Secretary deobligates or terminates under paragraph (1), competitively award the grant funds to another applicant, consistent with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Reporting and information requirements; internet disclosure
children.label: h Reporting and information requirements; internet disclosure
i
children.text: the amount of the grant;

children.label: ii
children.text: the use by the entity of the grant amounts; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the progress of the entity towards fulfilling the objectives for which the grant was awarded;

children.label: B
children.text: establish mechanisms to ensure appropriate use of, and compliance with respect to all terms regarding, grant funds awarded under the Program;

children.label: i
children.text: a list of each entity that has applied for a grant under the Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of each application described in clause (i), including the proposed purpose of each grant described in that clause;

children.label: iii
children.text: the status of each application described in clause (i), including whether the Assistant Secretary has awarded a grant with respect to the application and, if so, the amount of the grant;

children.label: iv
children.text: each report submitted by an entity under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: v
children.text: any other information that is sufficient to allow the public to understand and monitor grants awarded under the Program; and

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that any entity with respect to which an award is deobligated or terminated under subsection (g) may, in a timely manner, appeal or otherwise challenge that deobligation or termination, as applicable; and
may establish additional reporting and information requirements for any recipient of a grant under the Program.

children.heading: Supplement not supplant
children.label: i Supplement not supplant
children.text: A grant awarded to an entity under the Program shall supplement, not supplant, other Federal or State funds that have been made available to the entity to carry out activities described in this section.

children.label: A
children.text: providing technical support and assistance, including ensuring consistency in data reporting;

children.label: B
children.text: providing assistance to entities to prepare the applications of those entities with respect to grants awarded under this section;

children.label: C
children.text: developing the report required under ; andsection 1725(a) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: conducting outreach to entities that may be eligible to be awarded a grant under the Program regarding opportunities to apply for such a grant;

5 percent to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, Indian Tribes, Alaska Native entities, and Native Hawaiian organizations to allow those tribes, entities, and organizations to carry out the activities described in this section; and

1 percent to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States that is not a State to enable those entities to carry out the activities described in this section.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: k Rules
children.text: The Assistant Secretary may prescribe such rules as may be necessary to carry out this section.
$250,000,000 for each of the first 5 fiscal years in which funds are made available to carry out this section; and
such sums as may be necessary for each fiscal year after the end of the 5-fiscal year period described in paragraph (1).
